<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11490575]] Wikidata name: Shionjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shionjiyama Kofun | native_name =心合寺山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shionjiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639138888889|N|135.640875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yao|qid=Q490872}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|20.9|N|135|38|27.15|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the [[:en:Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]], which runs north and south as a watershed between [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shionjiyama Kohun, Yao04.jpg|Diorama showing original appearance 080515 Shionjiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Shionziyama kohun02.JPG|Top of the tumulus </gallery>

It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the [[:en:Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]], which runs north and south as a watershed between [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11490575]] Wikidata name: Shionjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shionjiyama Kofun | native_name =心合寺山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shionjiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639138888889|N|135.640875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yao|WD=Q490872}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|20.9|N|135|38|27.15|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the [[:en:Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]], which runs north and south as a watershed between [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shionjiyama Kohun, Yao04.jpg|Diorama showing original appearance 080515 Shionjiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Shionziyama kohun02.JPG|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the [[:en:Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]], which runs north and south as a watershed between [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11490575]] Wikidata name: Shionjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shionjiyama Kofun | native_name =心合寺山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shionjiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639138888889|N|135.640875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yao|WD=Q490872}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|20.9|N|135|38|27.15|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the [[:en:Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]], which runs north and south as a watershed between [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shionjiyama Kohun, Yao04.jpg|Diorama showing original appearance 080515 Shionjiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Shionziyama kohun02.JPG|Top of the tumulus </gallery>

The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11490575]] Wikidata name: Shionjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shionjiyama Kofun | native_name =心合寺山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shionjiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639138888889|N|135.640875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yao|WD=Q490872}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|20.9|N|135|38|27.15|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the [[:en:Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]], which runs north and south as a watershed between [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shionjiyama Kohun, Yao04.jpg|Diorama showing original appearance 080515 Shionjiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Shionziyama kohun02.JPG|Top of the tumulus </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11490575]] Wikidata name: Shionjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shionjiyama Kofun | native_name =心合寺山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shionjiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639138888889|N|135.640875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yao|WD=Q490872}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|20.9|N|135|38|27.15|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the [[:en:Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]], which runs north and south as a watershed between [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shionjiyama Kohun, Yao04.jpg|Diorama showing original appearance 080515 Shionjiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Shionziyama kohun02.JPG|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shionjiyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/yaokibun/0000044440.html Yao City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://racco-taiken.com/sionji/ Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Site Museum] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1463 Yao City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the [[:en:Mount Ikoma|Ikoma Mountains]], which runs north and south as a watershed between [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shionjiyama Kohun, Yao04.jpg|Diorama showing original appearance 080515 Shionjiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Shionziyama kohun02.JPG|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shionjiyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/yaokibun/0000044440.html Yao City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://racco-taiken.com/sionji/ Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Site Museum] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1463 Yao City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Judging from these {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Ikoma Mountains}}, which runs north and south as a watershed between {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shionjiyama Kohun, Yao04.jpg|Diorama showing original appearance 080515 Shionjiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Shionziyama kohun02.JPG|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Shionjiyama Kofun | native_name =心合寺山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shionjiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639138888889|N|135.640875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yao|WD=Q490872}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|20.9|N|135|38|27.15|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Ikoma Mountains}}, which runs north and south as a watershed between {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}s. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Shionjiyama Kofun | native_name =心合寺山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shionjiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639138888889|N|135.640875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yao|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|20.9|N|135|38|27.15|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka|1=Yao, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Ikoma Mountains|1=Mount Ikoma}}, which runs north and south as a watershed between {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[en:Shionjiyama Kofun]][[ja:心合寺山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Shionjiyama Kofun | native_name =心合寺山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shionjiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639138888889|N|135.640875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yao|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|20.9|N|135|38|27.15|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka|1=Yao, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Ikoma Mountains|1=Mount Ikoma}}, which runs north and south as a watershed between {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shionjiyama Kohun, Yao04.jpg|Diorama showing original appearance 080515 Shionjiyama Kofun aerial.jpg|Aerial photograph taken in 1975. Shionziyama kohun02.JPG|Top of the tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shionjiyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/yaokibun/0000044440.html Yao City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://racco-taiken.com/sionji/ Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Site Museum] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1463 Yao City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} | name = Shionjiyama Kofun | native_name =心合寺山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shionjiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shionjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639138888889|N|135.640875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yao|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|20.9|N|135|38|27.15|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shionjiyama Kofun'''|心合寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}, located in the Otake neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka|1=Yao, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1966 with the area under protection expanded in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163211 |title=心合寺山古墳|trans-title=Shionjiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shionjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a slope at an elevation of 30 meters, in the fan-shaped land at the western foot of the {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Ikoma Mountains|1=Mount Ikoma}}, which runs north and south as a watershed between {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}s. The tumulus has a total length of 160 meters, with a 92-meter diameter posterior circular portion, and is orientated to the south. Since it was built along the contour lines of a natural slope, the surrounding moat was divided into two embankments on the south and north sides, making it a rare structure with different water levels in the east and west. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of over 3000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', which included figurative, pot-shaped and "morning-glory-shaped" versions as well as the more common cylindrical ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> There was a protrusion on the west side of the central construction, forming a ceremonial platform.
At the top of the posterior circle, there are three [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s of different lengths, of which the remains of a clay-covered wooden coffin was confirmed in the west side. Various burial items such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', armor, iron swords, and iron spearheads were excavated from the surrounding area. Judging from these {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} and the construction method, the tumulus was compiled in the middle Kofun period, or around the 5th century. During the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} a Buddhist temple called Shingo-ji was constructed on the western side of the tumulus. The temple eventually disappeared, with its place name becoming distorted over time to become "Shinonji". The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1993, with further excavations occurring annually until 2003.
The tumulus and its surroundings are now maintained as an archaeological park and replicas of ''haniwa'' have been placed on the tumulus. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are displayed at the Yao City Shionjiyama Kofun Learning Center, located next to the tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from the "Otake" bus stop on the Kintetsu Bus from {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 160 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 90 meters wide x 12 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 92 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 3-tiers
